summary_one_line: Input must encode a well-formed pop3 URL with valid host:port so a live connection survives to the mid-transfer removal state.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A length-prefixed pop3 URL whose host/port/path bytes parse into a connectable scheme, driving the easy handle past MSTATE_DO into an active mid-transfer state with data->conn non-NULL when curl_multi_remove_handle runs (line 800 true branch).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry a clean structured 'pop3' scheme followed by a concentrated host/port literal (':::::::::900', '0.1:9001?850') and a HTTP/-like header block; the connection advances so data->conn is set and mstate is between MSTATE_DO and MSTATE_COMPLETED, and per-role coverage marks the true-branch body (lines 805/814) [W].
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds after the 'pop3'-region bytes degenerate into long runs of '%'/'+'/'.' filler (e.g. 'pk+++++*++' and '%%%%%%%%%%%%') that do not parse into a valid host:port, so the handle never reaches an active connection and the line-800 condition stays false (L hits the false-path msglist loop at lines 891-898).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads CMP operands captured during URL/scheme parsing and substitutes the exact byte sequences (scheme token, port digits, separators) into the input, building the precise host:port string that naive/value_profile cannot assemble by blind havoc; the I2S-floor dash row in the winning seed's lineage confirms an I2S contribution.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique (cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace over naive/value_profile).
The second decisive pair is cmplog > naive, also an I2S delta.
The blocked true branch at line 800 requires data->conn non-NULL plus an active mid-transfer mstate.
Winner coverage takes the true branch body (streamclose / multi_done at 805/814) while loser does not.
Winner seeds carry a clean structured pop3 URL with a host/port literal.
A winner cmplog seed encodes pop3 with an address:port string '0.1:9001'.
Loser seeds degenerate into '+' filler runs after the pop-region bytes, not a parseable host:port.
Loser seeds are dominated by repeated '%' filler bytes.
Winner bytes concentrate on structured URL/port characters at offsets 0x16-0x1e while loser bytes are diffuse filler.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes captured CMP operands at matching input offsets, the technique enabling the winner.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the loser side of pair 1 cannot do operand substitution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 303 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 2 --seed faf6b58d07527f1a` and observed one I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 22 of the 23-level ancestor chain, confirming an I2S contribution under the current build's dash-row floor signal.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the line-800 condition is gated only on a literal magic value with no structured host:port parse, yet cmplog still fails to beat naive/value_profile on resolving the true branch.

weakest_evidence_point: The mapping from input URL bytes to data->conn becoming non-NULL passes through multi_runsingle's connect state machine, which I infer from coverage rather than directly trace byte-to-pointer.
